name: Emily Kriner
school: Department of Astronomy and Planetary Science
focus: Planetary volcanology • Thermal infrared remote sensing • Surface evolution
bio: Emily Kriner is a planetary scientist at Northern Arizona University whose research focuses on remote sensing, volcanology, and planetary surface processes, particularly on Mars. Her work uses thermal infrared data and geospatial analysis to study volcanic features, surface composition, and geologic evolution on planetary bodies. She integrates spacecraft observations with analytical and modeling techniques to better understand how volcanic and surface processes shape planetary landscapes over time. Kriner’s research contributes to advancing interpretations of remote sensing data and improving our understanding of planetary geology and environmental history.
